page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a family. It features 4 dedicated virtual CPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, positioning the instance within the memory-optimized resource tier.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. There are zero integrated GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing tasks. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network configuration for public internet access.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from other tenants. The high memory density provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require significant RAM relative to compute power, avoiding the need to provision larger, more expensive instances just to meet memory thresholds. However, the lack of local storage and specialized CPU instruction flags represents a tradeoff for workloads dependent on local disk I/O or advanced vector and matrix operations.

The resource profile of this instance is suited for memory-intensive applications that do not require high-performance local storage or GPU acceleration. Typical workloads include small-to-medium database hosting, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and lightweight data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance in the c4a family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent performance without shared-core resource contention. However, it lacks local SSD storage, integrated GPUs, and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning all storage must be network-attached and public networking requires separate provisioning. The absence of advanced CPU instruction flags limits its suitability for specialized vector math or machine learning acceleration. Qualitatively, the instance offers balanced cost efficiency for applications requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources, such as medium-sized databases, caching layers, and enterprise application hosting.
